title: Microsoft Graph - Mail And Calendar APIs: Send, Read, Attachments, And Automation Patterns
applies_to: Sending formatted security report emails with attachments via Graph API
Reading and processing mailbox messages in automation workflows
Calendar event creation for reporting schedules and security reviews
Large attachment handling (upload session for files over 3MB)

confirmed_facts.fact: Send an email on behalf of a mailbox: `POST /users/{userId}/sendMail`. Body: `{ "message": { "subject": "...", "body": { "contentType": "HTML", "content": "<html>...</html>" }, "toRecipients": [{ "emailAddress": { "address": "user@domain.com" } }] }, "saveToSentItems": false }`. Requires Mail.Send application permission.

Attach small files (< 3MB) inline in sendMail: add an `attachments` array to the message object: `[{ "@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.fileAttachment", "name": "report.xlsx", "contentType": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "contentBytes": "<base64string>" }]`. Base64-encode the file content.

Large attachment upload (> 3MB, up to 150MB): create a draft message first: `POST /users/{userId}/messages` with the message body. Then create an upload session: `POST /users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}/attachments/createUploadSession` with `{ "AttachmentItem": { "attachmentType": "file", "name": "report.xlsx", "size": <byteLength> } }`. Upload in 320KB chunks. Then send: `POST /users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}/send`.

List messages in a mailbox folder: `GET /users/{userId}/mailFolders/Inbox/messages?$select=id,subject,from,receivedDateTime,isRead&$top=50&$orderby=receivedDateTime desc`. Filter by date: `$filter=receivedDateTime ge 2026-03-01T00:00:00Z`. Required permission: Mail.Read.

Create a calendar event: `POST /users/{userId}/events` with body `{ "subject": "Weekly Security Review", "start": { "dateTime": "2026-04-01T10:00:00", "timeZone": "UTC" }, "end": { "dateTime": "2026-04-01T11:00:00", "timeZone": "UTC" }, "attendees": [{"emailAddress":{"address":"user@domain.com"}, "type": "required"}], "isOnlineMeeting": true, "onlineMeetingProvider": "teamsForBusiness" }`. Requires Calendars.ReadWrite permission.

PowerShell sendMail via Graph SDK: `$mailParams = @{ Message = @{ Subject = 'Security Report'; Body = @{ ContentType = 'HTML'; Content = $htmlBody }; ToRecipients = @(@{ EmailAddress = @{ Address = 'recipient@domain.com' } }) }; SaveToSentItems = $false }; Send-MgUserMail -UserId $senderUpn -BodyParameter $mailParams`. Requires Microsoft.Graph.Mail sub-module.

Mail.Send application permission allows the app to send as ANY user in the tenant. To restrict sending to a specific mailbox, apply an Exchange application access policy: `New-ApplicationAccessPolicy -AppId $appId -PolicyScopeGroupId $mailEnabledSecurityGroup -AccessRight RestrictAccess`. Only mailboxes in the group can be accessed by the app.

implementation_notes: For the security reporting pipeline, send the report email as the last step. Use a dedicated shared mailbox (e.g., security-reports@domain.com) as the sender. Apply an application access policy to restrict Graph mail access to that mailbox only.
For Excel report attachments, prefer the large file upload session pattern even for files just above 3MB — inline base64 encoding inflates payload size by ~33% and can trigger request size limits.
For HTML email bodies, test rendering in Outlook Web Access before sending to recipients. Outlook strips many CSS properties; use inline styles and table-based layouts for reliable rendering.
Use `saveToSentItems: false` for automated pipeline emails to avoid filling the sender mailbox's sent items folder.
caveats: Mail.Send is a high-privilege permission. Always apply an Exchange application access policy to scope it to a specific mailbox group. Without this policy, the app can impersonate any user in the tenant.
The large attachment upload session URL is temporary (expires in ~24 hours). Complete the upload and send the message within the session lifetime.
Calendar event creation with `isOnlineMeeting: true` requires the user to have a Teams license assigned. Without a license, the Teams meeting link will not be generated.
